zh.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwer是Jan I de vivario Van de Wouwer的兒子，於1210年出生在比利時安特維爾。他和Beatrix nn至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1297年享年87歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwer是Jan I de vivario Van de Wouwer的儿子，于1210年出生在比利时安特維爾。他和Beatrix nn至少有4个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1297年享年87岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vivario
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Abelardo, Adali, Armida, Belen, Camila, Lourdes, Marcelo, Mario, Mercedes, Renato.  Italian Dante, Domenic, Leonardo.

Italian and Spanish: status name, ultimately from Latin vicarius ‘substitute’, for an official who carried out duties on behalf of an absentee office-holder or who deputized for a magistrate. In ecclesiastical usage, the word became specialized to denote a priest who carried out duties in a parish on behalf of a bishop.

variants.name: Vicari
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Sal, Angelo, Vito, Carlo, Luciano, Pietro, Rocco, Agostino, Antonio, Crispino, Domenico.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Vicario  .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: den
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄧, see   Deng  .
Chinese: possibly from the Romanization of certain Chinese personal names of some early Chinese immigrants in the US.

Origin undetermined. — Note: It is possible that in the population figure published by the US Census Bureau the Dutch case-inflected definite article de ‘the’, which forms first part of surnames such as Den Bleyker and Den Boer , is in some instances counted as an independent surname. Compare Ten .

variants.name: Deng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Hua, Jian, Jing, Wei, Wen, Yi, Ying, Chao, Fang, Gang, Hui, Min.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鄧: (i) from Deng (鄧), the name of a state (located in Dengxian in Henan province) granted to Man Ji, uncle of Wu Ding (a king of the Shang dynasty (1600 –1046 BC )). (ii) from Deng Bowen (鄧伯溫), head of an ancient state (located in present-day Shandong province) dating back to the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (iii) from Deng (鄧), a fief (located in Hubei province) granted to descendants of Zhong Kang, the 4th king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600 BC ). (iv) from Deng Cheng (鄧城, City of Deng), a town in the state of Cai (located in present-day Henan province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). (v) from Deng (鄧), a title of Li Tianyi, the 8th son of Li Yu (the last king of the state of Southern Tang (937–975 AD ) during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms (907–960) periods). The state of Southern Tang was annexed by Emperor Taizong of Song (939–997 AD ) in 975 AD , and the royal families of South Tang were hunted down. Li Tianyi's son fled and changed the surname (Li) to Deng (鄧), his father's title.

nameMeanings.name: Wouwer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: cognate of   Bouwman   ‘farmer’ and of German   Bauer  .

variants.name: Bouwman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Marinus, Cornelis, Gerrit, Gradus, Teunis.
Dutch:
derivative of the personal name Bouwe with the diminutive element -man, from Boudewijn, Dutch form of Baldwin ; compare Bouwens .

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Loving’.

en.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwer was born in 1210, in Antwerp, Belgium as the son of Jan I de vivario Van de Wouwer. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Beatrix nn. He died in 1297, at the age of 87.

fr.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwer est né en 1210, à Anvers, Belgique, fils de Jan I de vivario Van de Wouwer. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Beatrix nn. Il est décédé en 1297, à l’âge de 87 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwer wurde 1210 in Antwerpen, Belgien, als Sohn von Jan I de vivario Van de Wouwer geboren. Er hatte mit Beatrix nn mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1297 im Alter von 87 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwer è nato il nel 1210 nel Anversa, Belgio come figlio di Jan I de vivario Van de Wouwer. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Beatrix nn. Morì nel 1297, all'età di 87 anni.

ja.Antwerpen, België: ベルギーアントウエルペン
ja.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwerは1210年にベルギーアントウエルペンでJan I de vivario Van de Wouwerの息子として生まれた。彼はBeatrix nnとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1297年に87歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwer(은)는 1210년 벨기에 앤트워펜에서 Jan I de vivario Van de Wouwer의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Beatrix nn(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1297년 87세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwer nasceu em 1210, em Antuérpia, Bélgica filho de Jan I de vivario Van de Wouwer. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Beatrix nn. Ele faleceu em 1297, com 87 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwer родился в 1210 г. в местности антвирпен, Бельгия. Его отца звали Jan I de vivario Van de Wouwer. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Beatrix nn. Он умер в 1297 г. в возрасте 87 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jan II de Vivario van den Wouwer nació en 1210, en Amberes, Bélgica como hijo de Jan I de vivario Van de Wouwer. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Beatrix nn. Murió en 1297, a la edad de 87 años.
